PHASE 6: Infinite Loop Protection — Final Integration Scenarios (6 Tests) > Scenario 1: Simple Loop Prevention > Integration Test 1: Task fails 3 times with same fingerprint → 4th attempt blocked → stop reason MAX_RETRIES_EXCEEDED

PHASE 6: Infinite Loop Protection — Final Integration Scenarios (6 Tests) > Scenario 2: Timeout Enforcement > Integration Test 2: Agent executes for 5+ minutes → execution stops with EXECUTION_TIMEOUT → queue items preserved

PHASE 6: Infinite Loop Protection — Final Integration Scenarios (6 Tests) > Scenario 3: Failure Cascade > Integration Test 3: 10+ tasks fail in sequence → stop reason TOO_MANY_FAILURES → execution gracefully halts

PHASE 6: Infinite Loop Protection — Final Integration Scenarios (6 Tests) > Scenario 4: Executor Overload Recovery > Integration Test 4: All executor slots filled → new requests queued → slots released properly → queue processes released items

PHASE 6: Infinite Loop Protection — Final Integration Scenarios (6 Tests) > Scenario 5: Queue Cleanup Integration > Integration Test 5: Mix of RUNNING/COMPLETED/FAILED tasks → cleanup removes only safe items → protected items retained → DLQ receives stale pending items

Z3 Proof: Rate-Limit Window Invariants > within a window: remaining + used = limit (conservation theorem)
Z3 Proof: Rate-Limit Window Invariants > request is blocked iff used >= limit (no bypass theorem)
Z3 Proof: Rate-Limit Window Invariants > after increment: new_used = old_used + 1 (atomic counter theorem)
Z3 Proof: Rate-Limit Window Invariants > rate limit for auth (3/min email) is strictly less than checkout (20/min)
Z3 Proof: Stripe Pricing Arithmetic > yearly price < 12 × monthly price (discount invariant)
Z3 Proof: Stripe Pricing Arithmetic > yearly price = 9 × monthly price (exactly 3 months free, 25% discount — Z3 verified)
Z3 Proof: Stripe Pricing Arithmetic > all prices are positive integers (no zero-price exploit)
Z3 Proof: Quota Gate Safety (no double-spend) > check-before-increment prevents overspend by at most 1 under concurrency
Z3 Proof: Quota Gate Safety (no double-spend) > initialized counter + positive increments ≥ 0 (non-negative by construction)
Z3 Proof: Plan Transition Safety > valid upgrade paths are strictly increasing in rank
Z3 Proof: Plan Transition Safety > downgrade to free always results in rank 0
Z3 Proof: Quota Ordering Theorem > PLAN_QUOTA is strictly ordered: enterprise > business > pro > trial > free > 0
Z3 Proof: Quota Ordering Theorem > getQuotaForPlan never returns zero (safe floor invariant)
Z3 Proof: Quota Ordering Theorem > getQuotaForPlan(unknown) === FREE_QUOTA (safe floor for unknown plans)
Z3 Proof: Quota Ordering Theorem > getQuotaForPlan(null) === FREE_QUOTA
Z3 Proof: Billing Status Partition Theorem > ACTIVE_STATUSES and REVOKED_STATUSES are disjoint (no status can be both)
Z3 Proof: Billing Status Partition Theorem > effectivePlan returns planKey for every ACTIVE status
Z3 Proof: Billing Status Partition Theorem > effectivePlan returns "free" for every REVOKED status
Z3 Proof: Billing Status Partition Theorem > effectivePlan(null, null) === "free" (null-safety theorem)
Z3 Proof: Revenue Monotonicity Theorem > upgrading plan never decreases quota (∀ plan A→B: quota[B] ≥ quota[A])
Z3 Proof: Revenue Monotonicity Theorem > free plan quota (60) fits in an int32 without overflow
Z3 Proof: Revenue Monotonicity Theorem > enterprise plan quota fits in a JavaScript safe integer
Z3 Proof: Billing Period Arithmetic > billing period YYYY-MM format: month in [1..12]
Z3 Proof: Billing Period Arithmetic > Z3: any valid month m satisfies 1 ≤ m ≤ 12

testcase.name: Phase 1: Max Retries + Fingerprint + Break Condition > generateTaskFingerprint() > should generate a 16-char hex string
Phase 1: Max Retries + Fingerprint + Break Condition > generateTaskFingerprint() > should be deterministic: same input = same output
Phase 1: Max Retries + Fingerprint + Break Condition > generateTaskFingerprint() > should differ for different inputs
Phase 1: Max Retries + Fingerprint + Break Condition > recordTaskAttempt() > should create initial attempt with retryCount=0
Phase 1: Max Retries + Fingerprint + Break Condition > recordTaskAttempt() > should increment retryCount on subsequent calls
Phase 1: Max Retries + Fingerprint + Break Condition > recordTaskAttempt() > should track failure reasons
Phase 1: Max Retries + Fingerprint + Break Condition > recordTaskAttempt() > should record different failure reasons
Phase 1: Max Retries + Fingerprint + Break Condition > recordTaskAttempt() > should track timestamps
Phase 1: Max Retries + Fingerprint + Break Condition > canRetryTask() > should allow retry on first failure
Phase 1: Max Retries + Fingerprint + Break Condition > canRetryTask() > should allow up to 3 retries
Phase 1: Max Retries + Fingerprint + Break Condition > canRetryTask() > should block fingerprint after 3 identical failures
Phase 1: Max Retries + Fingerprint + Break Condition > canRetryTask() > should return stop reason on max retries
Phase 1: Max Retries + Fingerprint + Break Condition > blockFingerprint() > should mark fingerprint as blocked
Phase 1: Max Retries + Fingerprint + Break Condition > blockFingerprint() > should prevent retry after blocking
Phase 1: Max Retries + Fingerprint + Break Condition > getAttempt() > should return attempt for tracked task
Phase 1: Max Retries + Fingerprint + Break Condition > getAttempt() > should return undefined for untracked task
Phase 1: Max Retries + Fingerprint + Break Condition > clearAttempts() > should clear all attempt tracking
Phase 1: Max Retries + Fingerprint + Break Condition > Integration tests > should handle single failure + retry
Phase 1: Max Retries + Fingerprint + Break Condition > Integration tests > should block after 3 identical failures
Phase 1: Max Retries + Fingerprint + Break Condition > Integration tests > should differentiate between different tasks with same fingerprint

RequestQueue - Priority Levels and FIFO > should enqueue and dequeue with priority 1
RequestQueue - Priority Levels and FIFO > should enqueue and dequeue with priority 2
RequestQueue - Priority Levels and FIFO > should enqueue and dequeue with priority 3
RequestQueue - Priority Levels and FIFO > should prioritize P1 over P2 and P3
RequestQueue - Priority Levels and FIFO > should prioritize P2 over P3
RequestQueue - Priority Levels and FIFO > should maintain FIFO order within priority 1
RequestQueue - Priority Levels and FIFO > should maintain FIFO order within priority 2
RequestQueue - Priority Levels and FIFO > should maintain FIFO order within priority 3
RequestQueue - Fairness and No Starvation > should process P3 after max consecutive P3 dequeues (fairness limit)
RequestQueue - Fairness and No Starvation > should not starve P3 when P1 and P2 are available
RequestQueue - Fairness and No Starvation > should process P3 when P1 and P2 queues are empty
RequestQueue - Fairness and No Starvation > fairness counter resets when higher priority dequeued
RequestQueue - Timeout and Expiration > should enqueue request with 30 second deadline
RequestQueue - Timeout and Expiration > should skip expired requests during dequeue
RequestQueue - Timeout and Expiration > should count expired requests in stats
RequestQueue - Timeout and Expiration > should return null when all requests are expired
RequestQueue - Timeout and Expiration > should handle multiple expired requests before valid one
